journal_entry.description: Discounted 90-day non-interest-bearing note at bank, 15% annual discount rate, 40 days remaining
journal_entry.rationale: Ford discounts the note 50 days after issue, leaving 40 days to maturity. Bank discount = $100,000 × 15% × (40/365) = $1,643.84. Net proceeds = $100,000 - $1,643.84 = $98,356.16. Since management determined this qualifies as derecognition under IFRS 9, the note receivable is removed and the discount charge is recognized as a loss on disposal of financial asset (expense).

lines.account_name: Loss on Discounting of Note Receivable

output_debit_classifier.reason: Note discounting with derecognition: cash received (asset increase), loss on discount (expense increase), note receivable removed (asset decrease). Discount calculated as $100,000 × 15% × (40/365) = $1,643.84 for the 40 days remaining to maturity.
